# <Initiative / Feature Name> — BRD Draft

> Clarify draft at **business altitude**. Items marked `ASSUMPTION` /
> `OPEN QUESTION` / `SUGGESTION` need human confirmation. Clarify does not invent
> business rules. Deep technical mechanics are deferred to §19 Downstream
> Technical Notes — they are not BRD scope.

## Document Profile
<!-- Read by /clarify:finalize (by this heading) to pick the final document standard. -->
- Role: <BA | PO>
- Target standard: BRD
- Domain: <pack name | inferred (no pack — domain items below are labeled)>
- Language: <vi | en | …>   (all output renders in this language; labels
  ASSUMPTION/OPEN QUESTION/SUGGESTION and IDs stay English)

## 0. How to read this draft
### 0.1 What this is
A shaped BRD draft at business altitude. **Quick read:** skim §4 "How the system
works", §7 "Business requirements", and the open items (§13). Reply to the labeled
items via the Answer Sheet (§20).

### 0.2 Symbol conventions
Codes are **stable across versions**; the names beside them are only for reading.
| Symbol | Means | Example |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `F0n-Name` | A functional flow (number is the stable anchor; name is for reading) | `F02-Login` |
| `BRD-R#` | A business requirement | `BRD-R01` |
| `BR#` | A business rule | `BR3` |
| `A#` / `Q#` / `S#` / `V#` | Assumption / Open Question / Suggestion / Variant | `A1` / `Q2` / `S1` / `V1` |

### 0.3 Glossary
Define each domain term ONCE so the requirements below read plainly. Only define
terms the requirements actually use; do not invent terms.
| Term | Plain meaning |
| --- | --- |
| <e.g. login code> | <one-time code that ties a sign-in to a (user, app) pair> |
| <e.g. host-wide user id> | <a stable id for the user across the whole platform> |

## 4. How the system works (overview)
A plain end-to-end narrative so a first-time reader understands the whole before
the parts. Detail for each flow lives in §9.
1. <enters via menu / lands on …>
2. <chooses option / sees terms & key info>
3. <enters details / selects source & options>
4. <reviews preview: amount, dates, expected outcome>
5. <accepts terms> → <authenticates: OTP/PIN/biometric>
6. <system processes> → <result: success/failure>
7. <views detail / next action>

<Optionally add ONE representative diagram for the whole journey; detailed per-flow
diagrams belong in §9. At business altitude a textual flow is acceptable:>
```text
<Actor> <does step> → <system/business outcome> → <next step> → …
```

## 7. Business Requirements
**One grouped table (Principle 13.7).** Requirements grouped by capability in journey
order; within a group **Must → Should → Could**. Each is a self-standing business
sentence (trigger + actor + outcome); **Why** is the plain business reason (a
cross-reference like `Source: BR2` is **not** a Why). Flow / rule / test / source
links live in §18 Traceability — not here. IDs are stable (append, never renumber).
| ID | Requirement | Why | Priority |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Group A — <capability name>** | | | |
| BRD-R01 | <When/trigger>, <actor> <does / receives outcome>. | <plain business reason> | Must |
| BRD-R02 | <…> | <…> | Should |
| **Group B — <capability name>** | | | |
| BRD-R03 | <…> | <…> | Must |

## 9. Functional Flows (process-centric)
At business altitude: a flow catalog and the screen matrix. Flow IDs are `F0n-Name`
(number stable, name appended). Detailed PlantUML/Mermaid diagrams are produced by
`from-spec` / `improve model`; reference them here when they exist.

### 9.1 Flow Catalog
| Flow ID | Business name | Actor(s) | Goal | Related rules (BR) | Requirement (BRD-Rxx) |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| F01-<Name> | <…> | <…> | <…> | <…> | <BRD-R01> |

### 9.2 Screen Information / Display Matrix
What each key screen must show and let the user do (information level, not visual
design). Reference each screen's flow as `F0n-Name / step`. Include loading, empty,
validation, and error states where the user may be blocked or confused.
| Screen name | Flow / Step | Purpose | Display fields | User actions | Validation | Error / empty / loading states | UX / content notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| <product list> | F01-<Name> / 1 | <help user choose> | <product, rate/key terms, eligibility> | <select / compare> | <eligible products only?> | <no products / loading / unavailable> | <plain-language labels> |
| <confirmation summary> | F01-<Name> / 4 | <confirm commitment> | <amount, source, terms, dates, fees/penalty, terms checkbox> | <accept terms / authenticate / cancel> | <terms accepted, auth required> | <rate changed / auth unavailable> | <no technical jargon> |
| <result> | F01-<Name> / 6 | <show outcome> | <success/failure/pending, reference, next step> | <view detail / retry / contact support> | <n/a> | <unknown / processing / failed> | <message matches error map> |

<Business-level affected systems / source of truth. Detailed API/data impact is a

## 11. Edge cases, exception handling & messages
### 11.1 Error Handling & Customer Messages (summary)
Key failures with the **message the customer sees**, the state left behind, and the
next action (full code/status table in `error-handling.md`). Reference the
`F0n-Name / step` where each error occurs. User messages avoid technical jargon.
| Error code | Flow / Step | Scenario | Entity state | Transaction / operation state | User-facing message | Retryable? | Required action | Needs Ops/CS? |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| <CODE_001> | F01-<Name> / 3 | <insufficient balance> | <unchanged / none created> | <failed> | <plain message> | yes | <choose another source / amount> | no |
| <CODE_002> | F01-<Name> / auth | <auth failed / expired / cancelled> | <unchanged / pending> | <failed / cancelled> | <plain message> | yes/no | <retry / resend / restart> | no |

### 11.2 State Summary
For process / transaction features, distinguish the state of the business object
from the state of the action currently being processed. Mark unknowns `OPEN QUESTION`.
| Model type | State set | Key transitions / terminal states | Gaps |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Entity state | <e.g. pending, active, closed> | <allowed transitions> | <OPEN QUESTION / none> |
| Transaction / operation state | <initiated, pending-auth, processing, success, failed, timeout, reversed, unknown> | <auth → processing → final state> | <OPEN QUESTION / none> |

## 18. Traceability (draft)
The trace layer for the requirements above: every requirement once (including
deferred — keep the row, mark Status = Deferred), with its **Source** (the
`A#/BR#/S#/Q#` it came from). `finalize` carries this into the final §12.
| BRD-R id | Flow (F0n-Name) | Business rule | Source (← A#/BR#/S#/Q#) | Status |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| BRD-R01 | F01-<Name> | BR3 | A1 | Active |
| BRD-R14 | — | — | S2 | Deferred |

## 19. Downstream Technical Notes (for PRD / SRS — not BRD scope)
> Surfaced for traceability so nothing is lost; to be detailed in the PRD/SRS.
> Business reviewers may skip this section.
- Configuration mechanics: effective-dated/versioned config rows, validation, propagation.
- Scheduled/batch jobs: idempotency keys, resume-on-failure, outputs, downstream consumers.
- Data integrity: e.g. double-entry ledger, source of truth, reconciliation mechanics.
- API / event / integration impact.

## 20. Answer Sheet (copy, fill in, send back)
Copy this block, fill after each ID, and paste it back — `improve` applies it by ID.
```text
# Variant — pick one id (see §6):
Variant: V1
# Assumptions — keep | override: <value>
A1: keep
A2: keep
# Open questions — your answer (or "skip"):
Q1:
Q2:
# Suggestions — yes | no | later:
S1:
S2:
```
